canonical.name: soc-pokec
canonical.purpose: Slovak social network; ~1.6M nodes / 30M edges; the smallest of the social trio used for accuracy validation.

canonical.name: soc-LiveJournal1
canonical.purpose: Friend-of-friend social graph; ~4.85M nodes / 69M edges; primary mid-scale social regression target.

canonical.name: com-orkut
canonical.purpose: Community membership graph; ~3M nodes / 117M edges; highest-density social graph in the corpus.

canonical.name: com-orkut-undir
canonical.purpose: Symmetrised (undirected) variant of com-orkut used by lit_faith_diversity for parity with literature comparisons that explicitly assume undirected community membership.

canonical.name: email-Eu-core
canonical.purpose: Email exchange in an EU research org; ~1K nodes / 16K edges; the smoke-test / micro-benchmark graph used by lit_faith_accesses (SMOKE_GRAPHS).

canonical.name: cit-Patents
canonical.purpose: US patent citation network; ~3.8M nodes / 16.5M edges; low-density, citation-family canary.

canonical.name: web-Google
canonical.purpose: Google's web crawl; ~916K nodes / 4.3M edges; primary web-family regression target with mid hub concentration.

canonical.name: web-BerkStan
canonical.purpose: Berkeley + Stanford web crawl; ~685K nodes / 7.6M edges; small-to-mid web-family graph used by lit_faith_diversity for web-family sensitivity across multiple crawls.

canonical.name: p2p-Gnutella31
canonical.purpose: Gnutella peer-to-peer file-sharing topology (Aug 31, 2002); ~63K nodes / 148K edges; the lone p2p-family canary used by lit_faith_diversity to ensure family coverage extends beyond social/web/road/mesh/citation.

canonical.name: roadNet-CA
canonical.purpose: California state road network; ~2M nodes / 5.5M edges; low-degree, planar canary that stresses GRASP's hub heuristic (no hubs to find).

canonical.name: roadNet-PA
canonical.purpose: Pennsylvania state road network; SNAP companion to roadNet-CA; used by lit_faith_margin as a road-family sensitivity check across multiple state graphs.

canonical.name: roadNet-TX
canonical.purpose: Texas state road network; SNAP companion to roadNet-CA; used by lit_faith_margin as a road-family sensitivity check across multiple state graphs.

canonical.name: road-CA
canonical.purpose: Kebab-case display variant of roadNet-CA used by paper tables that strip the camel-case 'Net' suffix for compact reporting; declared so the family-classifier dicts in policy_winner_table parity tests can lock the spelling without R4 violations.

canonical.name: USA-Road
canonical.purpose: Continental US road network (DIMACS 9th); ~24M nodes / 58M edges; large-scale road-family target referenced by EVAL_GRAPHS in config.py.

canonical.name: wikipedia_link_en
canonical.purpose: English Wikipedia internal link graph; ~12M nodes / 378M edges; largest evaluation graph for sustained-throughput comparisons against the literature.

canonical.name: delaunay_n18
canonical.purpose: Delaunay triangulation of 2^18 random 2-D points; small-scale variant used by lit_faith_margin as a mesh-family scaling sensitivity check (companion to n19/n20).

canonical.name: delaunay_n19
canonical.purpose: Delaunay triangulation of 2^19 random 2-D points; ~524K nodes / 3.1M edges; planar, low-degree, no hubs — the synthetic complement to roadNet-CA.

canonical.name: delaunay_n20
canonical.purpose: Delaunay triangulation of 2^20 random 2-D points; mid-scale variant used by lit_faith_margin as a mesh-family scaling sensitivity check (companion to n18/n19).

canonical.name: kron21
canonical.purpose: Kronecker R-MAT generator with scale 21 (~2M nodes); RESERVED_FUTURE_KEYS in lit_faith_graph_family; declared for future scale-up sweeps against the GAPBS reference.

canonical.name: kron22
canonical.purpose: Kronecker R-MAT generator with scale 22 (~4M nodes); RESERVED_FUTURE_KEYS in lit_faith_graph_family; declared for future scale-up sweeps against the GAPBS reference.

canonical.name: kron23
canonical.purpose: Kronecker R-MAT generator with scale 23 (~8M nodes); RESERVED_FUTURE_KEYS in lit_faith_graph_family; declared for future scale-up sweeps against the GAPBS reference.

canonical.name: soc-LJ
canonical.purpose: Compact paper-label alias of soc-LiveJournal1 used by RESERVED_FUTURE_KEYS in lit_faith_graph_family; the short form preferred by some baseline tables.

canonical.name: twitter7
canonical.purpose: Compact alias of the 2010 Twitter snapshot (~52M nodes / 1.96B edges); RESERVED_FUTURE_KEYS form used by some literature tables under the shorter spelling.

canonical.name: web-uk
canonical.purpose: Compact alias of uk-2005 (~39M nodes / 936M edges); RESERVED_FUTURE_KEYS form used by some literature tables under the shorter spelling.

canonical.name: twitter-2010
canonical.purpose: WebGraph snapshot of the 2010 Twitter follow network; ~42M nodes / 1.5B edges; large-scale social-family regression target referenced by policy_winner_table parity tests against the GRASP/POPT literature.

canonical.name: uk-2005
canonical.purpose: WebGraph snapshot of the .uk top-level domain (2005); ~39M nodes / 936M edges; large-scale web-family regression target referenced by policy_winner_table parity tests against the GRASP/POPT literature.

rules.R1: every harvested graph literal is in CANONICAL_GRAPHS
rules.R2: every canonical has non-empty family + paper_label
rules.R4: every family-classifier dict has canonical keys with matching families
rules.R5: every harvested literal has a non-family-dict site (unless documented_future=True)
rules.R7: every EVAL_GRAPHS entry in config.py is canonical
